explicit_text: I work as a registered nurse at Duke University Hospital, specifically in the ambulatory hospital-based clinic division. During the COVID-19 pandemic, Duke University Hospital opened a COVID tent early on, around February to April, where I volunteered to help with testing. It was a pivotal place where I saw patients of all ages come through, and it was a way for me to contribute directly during the crisis. The hospital has been central to our community's response and care throughout this challenging time.
implicit_text: I work as a registered nurse at a major academic medical center in Durham, specifically in the ambulatory hospital-based clinic division. During the early months of the COVID crisis, this hospital opened a COVID tent around February to April, where I volunteered to help with testing. It was a pivotal place where I saw patients of all ages come through, and it was a way for me to contribute directly during the crisis. This medical center has been central to our community's response and care throughout this challenging time.

explicit_text: I work at the Duke Outpatient Clinic located on Roxboro Road in Durham. This clinic is part of the ambulatory hospital-based division where I manage and support my team. Roxboro Road has been the site where much of our outpatient care and adaptations during the pandemic have taken place. It’s where we shifted from mostly in-person visits to telephone and video visits to keep patients safe.
implicit_text: I work at an outpatient clinic located on a well-known road in Durham. This clinic is part of the ambulatory hospital-based division where I manage and support my team. That road has been the site where much of our outpatient care and adaptations during the pandemic have taken place. It’s where we shifted from mostly in-person visits to telephone and video visits to keep patients safe.

explicit_text: I live and work in Durham, North Carolina, where the pandemic has deeply affected our community and healthcare system. Durham is home to Duke University Hospital and several clinics where I’ve worked throughout the COVID-19 crisis. The city has seen efforts like vaccine clinics at the Karsh Center and Duke Primary Care at Durham Medical Center, aiming to serve diverse populations including those facing barriers to healthcare.
implicit_text: I live and work in a city in North Carolina that has been deeply affected by the pandemic and its impact on our community and healthcare system. This city is home to a major academic medical center and several clinics where I’ve worked throughout the COVID-19 crisis. The city has seen efforts like vaccine clinics at a local community center and primary care clinics at the medical center, aiming to serve diverse populations including those facing barriers to healthcare.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has been a historic and challenging time in my nursing career. Starting in early 2020, it forced us to rapidly change how we deliver care, from in-person visits to telephone and video consultations. I volunteered at the COVID testing tent and vaccine clinics, witnessing firsthand the community’s resilience and gratitude. This pandemic also meant many meetings via Zoom and constant communication to keep staff and patients informed during a rapidly evolving situation.
implicit_text: The global viral outbreak that began in early 2020 has been a historic and challenging time in my nursing career. It forced us to rapidly change how we deliver care, from in-person visits to telephone and video consultations. I volunteered at the testing tent and vaccine clinics, witnessing firsthand the community’s resilience and gratitude. This crisis also meant many meetings via video conferencing and constant communication to keep staff and patients informed during a rapidly evolving situation.

explicit_text: Zoom became an essential tool for me during the pandemic as I shifted from attending in-person meetings at the hospital to virtual meetings. Starting my day early, I would join multiple Zoom huddles with leadership and my team to stay updated on staffing and patient care changes. It helped us maintain communication and adapt quickly to the evolving healthcare landscape. Zoom truly became my friend in managing the daily flow of information and team coordination.
implicit_text: A popular video conferencing platform became an essential tool for me during the pandemic as I shifted from attending in-person meetings at the hospital to virtual meetings. Starting my day early, I would join multiple video huddles with leadership and my team to stay updated on staffing and patient care changes. It helped us maintain communication and adapt quickly to the evolving healthcare landscape. This platform truly became my friend in managing the daily flow of information and team coordination.

explicit_text: I volunteered at the Karsh Center, where the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine was administered early in the vaccine rollout. It was inspiring to see people, especially older adults, arrive early and express gratitude for receiving the vaccine. Being part of that effort gave me a sense of pride and hope, knowing we were protecting our community. I also personally received the Pfizer vaccine early on after frontline workers had their chance.
implicit_text: I volunteered at a local community center, where one of the earliest authorized COVID-19 vaccines was administered during the initial rollout. It was inspiring to see people, especially older adults, arrive early and express gratitude for receiving the vaccine. Being part of that effort gave me a sense of pride and hope, knowing we were protecting our community. I also personally received that vaccine early on after frontline workers had their chance.

explicit_text: The Karsh Center was one of the key vaccination sites during the pandemic where I volunteered to help administer the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine. It was a place filled with gratitude and hope as many community members, including those with mobility challenges, came to get vaccinated. Working there allowed me to connect with the community in a meaningful way and see firsthand the impact of vaccination efforts. It was an uplifting experience during a difficult time.
implicit_text: A community center in Durham was one of the key vaccination sites during the pandemic where I volunteered to help administer one of the first COVID-19 vaccines. It was a place filled with gratitude and hope as many community members, including those with mobility challenges, came to get vaccinated. Working there allowed me to connect with the community in a meaningful way and see firsthand the impact of vaccination efforts. It was an uplifting experience during a difficult time.

explicit_text: Duke Primary Care at the Durham Medical Center hosted Moderna COVID-19 vaccine clinics on Saturdays, where I volunteered to help vaccinate patients. This clinic focused on reaching disenfranchised populations who faced barriers like transportation. It was rewarding to see many Brown patients and seniors come in, grateful for the opportunity to get vaccinated. Being part of Duke Primary Care’s outreach efforts made me feel like I was making a real difference in addressing healthcare disparities.

explicit_text: Durham Medical Center is where Duke Primary Care runs vaccine clinics, including the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine clinics I volunteered at. It serves many patients who face challenges accessing healthcare, so these clinics were vital in reaching vulnerable populations. I saw a lot of older African American and Brown patients come through, and it was rewarding to help provide them with vaccines to protect themselves and their families. The Medical Center has been a hub for community health efforts during the pandemic.

explicit_text: I volunteered at clinics administering the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine, particularly at Duke Primary Care in Durham Medical Center. These clinics targeted patients who often face barriers to healthcare, such as transportation issues. It was heartening to see many older Brown patients come in, thankful for the vaccine and hopeful it would help protect their communities. My own daughter received the Moderna vaccine, so it’s been a part of my family’s experience as well.

explicit_text: While I personally received the Pfizer vaccine and my daughter got Moderna, my son is planning to get the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine. It’s interesting to see how my family is experiencing all three vaccines and observing their effects. This variety within my family highlights the different options available and the importance of vaccination in protecting ourselves and others during the pandemic.
implicit_text: While I personally received the first mRNA vaccine and my daughter got the second mRNA vaccine, my son is planning to get the single-dose viral vector COVID-19 vaccine. It’s interesting to see how my family is experiencing all three vaccines and observing their effects. This variety within my family highlights the different options available and the importance of vaccination in protecting ourselves and others during the pandemic.
